title: WHO Guideline for Pharmacological Treatment of Hypertension
content: Adults with hypertension should be offered drug treatment when systolic BP is 140 mmHg or higher and/or diastolic BP is 90 mmHg or higher. First-line options include ACE inhibitors, angiotensin receptor blockers (ARBs), calcium channel blockers (CCBs), or thiazide diuretics. In most adults, two-drug combination (preferably as a single pill) is recommended when BP is more than 20/10 mmHg above target. Target BP is less than 140/90 mmHg for most adults; for those with cardiovascular disease or high risk, target can be 130/80 mmHg if tolerated. Lifestyle modifications (salt reduction, healthy diet, physical activity, weight management) should be encouraged alongside pharmacotherapy.
